DEFENDANT DEWEY SELPH, HEARST COMMUNICATIONS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO THIRD SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES RELATED REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SERVED THE SUBJECT INTERROGATORIES IN SEPTEMBER 2014, AND THEY HAVE NOT YET BEEN ANSWERED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S JUSTIFICATION - THAT PLAINTIFF IS "IMPOSSIBLE TO GET A HOLD OF AND PIN DOWN TO HELP WITH THE DISCOVERY" IS INADEQUATE, ESPECIALLY WHERE DISCOVERY HAS BEEN OUTSTANDING FOR SUCH A LONG PERIOD OF TIME. ACCORDINGLY, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS TO GRANT THE MOTION AND TO IMPOSE SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL IN THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF $1,860. PREVALING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM ANTHONY SCHOENBERG, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-14-2015 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF LOURDES ESCALANTE'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS AND DEMAND FOR INSPECTIONS, THE MOTIONS ARE GRANTED AS FOLLOWS. THE COURT ORDERS THE FOLLOWING: (1) DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE COMPLETE OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES TO THE WRITTEN DISCOVERY REQUESTS BY OCTOBER 23, 2015; (2) DEFENDANT AND PENSKE TO PRODUCE THEIR PMQS FOR DEPOSITION BY OCTOBER 23, 2015. PARTIES AND PENSKE TO MEET AND CONFER TO DECIDE ON A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TIME AND DATE FOR THE DEPOSITIONS AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. THERE HAS BEEN NO WAIVER REGARDING THE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE TO BE PRODUCED AT THE DEPOSITIONS; (3) DEFENDANT SHALL ALLOW A SECOND INSPECTION TO GO FORWARD, AS AGREED TO IN EX. N TO THE BRAVO DECLARATION, BY OCTOBER 23, 2015. DEFENDANT TO ALLOW INSPECTION OF THE ORIGINAL VAN OR IF UNAVAILABLE, A SIMILAR VAN. MR. SELPH DOES NOT NEED TO APPEAR AT THE INSPECTION; (4) DEFENDANTS TO PAY A TOTAL OF $1,200 FOR BOTH MOTIONS AS REASONABLE SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY NOVEMBER 2, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. AS TO PLAINTIFF LOURDES ESCALANTE'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND FOR SANCTIONS, THE MOTIONS ARE GRANTED AS FOLLOWS. THE COURT ORDERS THE FOLLOWING: (1) DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE COMPLETE OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES TO THE WRITTEN DISCOVERY REQUESTS BY OCTOBER 23, 2015; (2) DEFENDANT AND PENSKE TO PRODUCE THEIR PMQS FOR DEPOSITION BY OCTOBER 23, 2015. PARTIES AND PENSKE TO MEET AND CONFER TO DECIDE ON A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TIME AND DATE FOR THE DEPOSITIONS AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. THERE HAS BEEN NO WAIVER REGARDING THE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE TO BE PRODUCED AT THE DEPOSITIONS; (3) DEFENDANT SHALL ALLOW A SECOND INSPECTION TO GO FORWARD, AS AGREED TO IN EX. N TO THE BRAVO DECLARATION, BY OCTOBER 23, 2015. DEFENDANT TO ALLOW INSPECTION OF THE ORIGINAL VAN OR IF UNAVAILABLE, A SIMILAR VAN. MR. SELPH DOES NOT NEED TO APPEAR AT THE INSPECTION; (4) DEFENDANTS TO PAY A TOTAL OF $1,200 FOR BOTH MOTIONS AS REASONABLE SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY NOVEMBER 2, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Continue Trial
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DORIANNE ROMERO PLIHON, ESQ. of Bravo & Margulies, Tel No. 415-512-6700 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF LOURDES ESCALANTE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions, the motions are granted as follows. The court orders the following: (1) Defendants shall provide complete objection-free responses to the written discovery requests by October 23, 2015; (2) Defendant and Penske to produce their PMQs for deposition by October 23, 2015. Parties and Penske to meet and confer to decide on a mutually convenient time and date for the depositions as soon as possible. There has been no waiver regarding the documents that are to be produced at the depositions; (3) Defendant shall allow a second inspection to go forward, as agreed to in Ex. N to the Bravo declaration, by October 23, 2015. Defendant to allow inspection of the original van or if unavailable, a similar van. Mr. Selph does not need to appear at the inspection; (4) Defendants to pay a total of $1,200 for both motions as reasonable sanctions to plaintiff's counsel by November 2, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Mr. Selph does not need to appear at the inspection; (4) Defendants to pay a total of $1,200 for both motions as reasonable sanctions to plaintiff's counsel by November 2, 2015. MINI MINUTES:

The following motions: 1) PLAINTIFF LOURDES ESCALANTE'S Motion To Compel Depositions And Demand For Inspections and 2) PLAINTIFF LOURDES ESCALANTE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions are continued to 10/15/15 on the court's motion to be heard in the law and motion department (Room 302) at 9:30 a.m. The Judge Pro Tem recuses himself. (302)

Notice Of Defendants Motion To Compel Responses To Third Set Of Special Interrogatories Related Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Joseph Bravo of Bravo & Margulies (415)512-6700 appeard for plaintiff Lourdes Escalante opposing party. / Atty. Laura Flynn of Low, Ball & Lynn (415)981-6630 appeard for defendants Dewey Selph and Hearst Communications, moving party.

Argued, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DEWEY SELPH, HEARST COMMUNICATIONS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Third Set Of Special Interrogatories Related Request For Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants served the subject interrogatories in September 2014, and they have not yet been answered. Plaintiff's counsel's justification - that Plaintiff is "impossible to get a hold of and pin down to help with the discovery" is inadequate, especially where discovery has been outstanding for such a long period of time. Accordingly, the tentative ruling is to GRANT the motion and to impose sanctions against Plaintiff and her counsel in the total amount of $1,860. Prevaling party to prepare order. Judge Pro Tem Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)
